keyTerms.Luftwaffe: The German air force under Hermann Göring, tasked with destroying the RAF and terrorizing British civilians during the Blitz
keyTerms.spirit of the Blitz: The popular narrative of defiant civilian solidarity under bombing, celebrated by Churchill and amplified by wartime propaganda
keyTerms.Ministry of Information: The British government department that controlled wartime news, images, and propaganda, suppressing evidence of panic and chaos
keyTerms.Tube shelters: London Underground stations that became vast unofficial dormitories for up to 177,000 people per night despite initial government prohibition
keyTerms.East End: The working-class docklands district of London that bore the brunt of the bombing due to its industrial and maritime targets
